sub_problems: How should multiple independent budget/state signals (compute, latency, safety, freshness, anomaly) be combined into a single scalar aperture without losing the multi-signal structure?
What is the right speed/inertia of the aperture — too fast and it oscillates; too slow and it lags behind real-time constraint changes?

How does the aperture interact with the agent's attention mechanism — is it pre-attention (filters tokens before attention), in-attention (modifies attention weights), or post-attention (filters output before generation)?
What is the failure mode when multiple signals conflict (e.g., safety wants closed, freshness wants open), and what arbitration rule governs that conflict?
